title: Spectrophotometric and Conductometric Studies of the Complexes between UO 22+ and 2-liydroxy-3-naphthoic Acid in Aqueous Medium
author: S. L. Gupta
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The orange-coloured, water-soluble complex, formed by the interaction of uranyl ion and 2-hydroxy­-3-nahthoie sold, has been studied speetrophotometrically in aqueous solutions of different ionic strengths and at different temperatures. It has an absorption maximum at 450 mp. and a flat region thereafter. The optimum pH range le 4.5 to 4.8 and it is stable towards time. The formula of the complex, established spectre­photemetrioally and conductometrically, is UO 2 R (R= reagent). The molecular extinction coefficient Is 4.18X 10'. The dissociation Constant of the oomph's has been determined for different ionic strengths and temperatures. The values of All and As are found to be —5.104 kcal. and 37.2 ± 0.2 e.e. respectively. The probable structure of the complex is also suggeted.
